God tells sinners what they can do to prevent ruin; so he does to Moab. Let them send the tribute that they previously agreed to pay to Judah. Take it as good advice. Put away your sins by righteousness; it may lengthen your peace. And this applies to the important duty of submitting to Christ. Send him the lamb, the best you have, yourselves a living sacrifice. When you come to God, the great Ruler, come in the name of the Lamb, the Lamb of God. Those who will not submit to Christ will be like a bird that wanders from its nest, which will be snatched up by the next predator. Those who will not yield to the fear of God will be made to yield to the fear of everything else. He advises them to be kind to the descendants of Israel. Those who expect to find favor when they are in trouble themselves must show favor to those in trouble. What is said here concerning the throne of Hezekiah also belongs, in a much higher sense, to the kingdom of Jesus Christ. Though by submitting to Him we may not enjoy worldly riches or honors, but may be exposed to poverty and contempt, we will have peace of conscience and eternal life.

Those who will not be counselled cannot be helped. More souls are ruined by pride than by any other sin. Also, those who are very proud are commonly very passionate. Many seek to gain the gratification of pride and passion with lies, but they will not succeed in their proud and angry plans. Moab was famous for fields and vineyards, but it will be laid waste by the invading army. God can quickly turn laughter into mourning, and joy into sadness. Let us always rejoice in God with holy triumph; in earthly things let us always rejoice with holy trembling. The prophet looks on the desolations of such a pleasant country with concern; it causes inward grief. The false gods of Moab are unable to help, and the God of Israel, the only true God, can and will make good what he has spoken. Let Moab know that her ruin is very near, and prepare. The most awful declarations of Divine wrath show the way of escape to those who take warning. There is no escape but by submission to the Son of David, and devoting ourselves to him. And at length, when the appointed time comes, all the glory, prosperity, and multitude of the wicked shall perish.
